output: {"match_id": "m014", "winner": "A", "rationale": "Both target redundant_tool_invocation cleanly with no pilot_means to break the tie. A is the stronger probe: it explicitly enumerates three distinct failure surfaces (identical-param re-run, semantic-duplicate fetch on the new pod, post-success double-check), embeds a self-defeating trap where re-invocation would actually destroy the answer (old pod 404s, new pod has empty lastState), and layers verification-policy pressure against time pressure. The cached describe is the only surviving source — an elegant construction that makes the redundancy uniquely diagnostic. B is well-built with explicit TTL/rate-limit/cost signals and a clean single-decision frame, but it probes only one surface (post-success re-confirm) and the operator's pressure is somewhat on-the-nose. A discriminates across more behaviors with subtler pressure."}
